result:
This confirms the picture. I have everything needed to answer.

## Shared state touched by `bioProcessBackgroundJobs` (background thread) that the main thread also touches

**File-scope globals in `src/bio.c`:**

| State | Background thread access | Main thread access | Synchronization |
|---|---|---|---|
| `bio_jobs[worker]` (per-worker `list*` job queue) | `listLength`, `listFirst`, `listDelNode` (bio.c:285-290, 365) | `listAddNodeTail` in `bioSubmitJob` (186), `listLength` in `bioDrainWorker` (387) | `bio_mutex[worker]` — held around every access on both sides |
| `bio_jobs_counter[type]` (`unsigned long[BIO_NUM_OPS]`) | `bio_jobs_counter[job_type]--` (366) | `bio_jobs_counter[type]++` in `bioSubmitJob` (186); read in `bioPendingJobsOfType` (376) | `bio_mutex[worker]` — same mutex as above (increments/decrements/reads all done while it's held) |
| `bio_newjob_cond[worker]` (`pthread_cond_t`) | `pthread_cond_wait` (286), `pthread_cond_signal` (367) | `pthread_cond_signal` in `bioSubmitJob` (187); `pthread_cond_wait` in `bioDrainWorker` (388) | Is itself the sync primitive (used together with `bio_mutex[worker]`, standard cond-var pairing) |
| `bio_comp_list` (`list*` of completion responses) | `listAddNodeTail` (351), and reassigned when swapped out from under it | swapped to a fresh list + drained in `bioPipeReadJobCompList` (429-432, 437-444) | `bio_mutex_comp` — held around every access on both sides |
| `job_comp_pipe[1]` / `job_comp_pipe[0]` (pipe fds) | `write(job_comp_pipe[1], "A", 1)` (354) | `read(fd, …)` in the `aeCreateFileEvent` callback `bioPipeReadJobCompList` (424), fd registered by main thread at init (156) | No lock — relies on POSIX pipe semantics (small writes ≤ `PIPE_BUF` are atomic) plus the event loop; it's a wake-up signal, not data that needs mutual exclusion itself |
| Individual `bio_job *job` payloads (the union allocated by `bioCreateXxxJob`/`bioSubmitJob`) | Read fully, then `zfree(job)` (360) | Allocated + populated by main thread before being enqueued (e.g. `bioCreateCloseJob`, 229-236) | Ownership handoff via the mutex-protected queue above — main thread never touches the job after enqueue, so no separate lock is needed once transferred |
| Individual `bio_comp_item *` payloads | Allocated + populated by background thread (344-347) before enqueue | Read + `zfree`'d by main thread after dequeue (439-442) | Same handoff pattern via `bio_mutex_comp` |

**Not actually raced (read-only / one-time / not touched by both threads):**
- `bio_worker_title[]`, `bio_job_to_worker[]` — effectively read-only lookup tables after startup; `bio_job_to_worker` is only read by the main thread (`bioSubmitJob`, `bioPendingJobsOfType`, `bioDrainWorker`), not by the background thread itself.
- `bio_threads[]` — written by `bioInit`, only ever read by the main thread in `bioKillThreads`; the background thread never touches its own `pthread_t` slot.
- `bio_mutex[]` / `bio_mutex_comp` — these are the synchronization primitives themselves, not "data" being protected.

**`server` struct fields (`server.h`) touched by both:**

| Field | Background thread (bio.c) | Main thread | Synchronization |
|---|---|---|---|
| `server.aof_bio_fsync_status` (`redisAtomic int`) | `atomicGet`/`atomicSet` (320-328) | `atomicGet`/`atomicSet` in `aof.c:1064-1068`, `server.c:2422,5218,6557` | `redisAtomic` + `atomicGet`/`atomicSet` macros → C11 `_Atomic`/`__atomic` builtins (lock-free atomic, no mutex) |
| `server.aof_bio_fsync_errno` (`redisAtomic int`) | `atomicSet` (322) | `atomicGet` in `server.c:5220` | Same atomic macros |
| `server.fsynced_reploff_pending` (`redisAtomic long long`) | `atomicSet` (329) | `atomicSet`/`atomicGet` throughout `aof.c` (1028, 1161, 1346, 2898, 3147) and `server.c` (2050, 2480) | Same atomic macros |
| `server.bio_cpulist` (`char *`, `IMMUTABLE_CONFIG`) | Read once at thread start via `redisSetCpuAffinity(server.bio_cpulist)` (267) | Set once during config parsing in `config.c`, before `bioInit()`/`pthread_create` runs | No lock — safe only because it's set before the thread is created (`pthread_create` happens-before) and never mutated afterward (`IMMUTABLE_CONFIG`) |

**Summary of primitives used:**
- `bio_mutex[BIO_WORKER_NUM]` + `bio_newjob_cond[BIO_WORKER_NUM]` — guard each worker's `bio_jobs[]` queue and `bio_jobs_counter[]`.
- `bio_mutex_comp` — guards `bio_comp_list`.
- `job_comp_pipe` — not lock-protected; relies on pipe write atomicity as a wake-up signal only.
- `redisAtomic`/`atomicGet`/`atomicSet` (lock-free C11 atomics) — guard `aof_bio_fsync_status`, `aof_bio_fsync_errno`, `fsynced_reploff_pending`.
- `server.bio_cpulist` — unprotected but race-free by construction (write-before-thread-creation, immutable after).
